comment: SUR-842 native-parity coverage manifest. One row per registered sync behavior in vendored/native-parity/sync-surface.json (the SUR-845 registry snapshot vendored from surfc/main). Source of truth for 'is this behavior accounted for natively'. status: core (handled in the shared braird-core layer, inherited by all native clients) | ios | android | waived. A waived row REQUIRES a non-empty note; a non-waived row REQUIRES a ticket (SUR-nnn). scripts/check-native-parity.mjs enforces one row per registered id (and no orphans) against surfc/main. Seed proposed from the port tickets 2026-07-11; ratify the RATIFY-flagged rows.

entries.note: Core sync engine — outbox flush-before-pull (SUR-659 / SUR-724 outbox push).
Core sync engine — incremental change_seq pull + LWW merge (SUR-659 / SUR-725).
Core post-pull reconciliation — fetch books a live note references but the pull did not deliver.
Core post-pull reconciliation — rehome notes off merged-away books onto the survivor.
Core post-pull reconciliation — dropped canon leaf -> user-scoped custom idea (SUR-597 behavior, homed to core in SUR-820).

Image + ink-crop BLOB download/hydration is host-side, NOT shared-core: core carries image_path/ink_crop_path as synced COLUMNS only — src/sync/reconcile.rs explicitly scopes 'image download/cache stays host-side (SUR-768/SUR-821), out of scope here'. Per-platform host work: Android SUR-768, iOS SUR-821. RATIFY: waiver-as-host-side, or split into per-platform rows.
Core sync engine — decrypt-on-read reload so app state holds plaintext.
Core sync engine — advance per-table change_seq cursors after a clean pull (SUR-739).

FULLY covered — both halves of the PWA's reconcileContentTags now land in core post-pull reconciliation. (1) exact-duplicate cluster collapse: reconcile_content_dupes (reconcile.rs, SUR-835, core PR #34) collapses live notes sharing a STORED content_tag into one deterministic survivor (most tags, then earliest created_at, then lowest id) with the full mergeNotes port (tags/image/note_links/collection_memberships), staged through the outbox. (2) load-time content_tag SELF-HEAL: reconcile_heal_content_tags (reconcile.rs, SUR-884) re-derives a null/missing tag from the note's decrypted text (SUR-638 HMAC) so a note whose tag was nulled by reconcile_stranded_notes (rehome/detach) is re-tagged and clustered WITHOUT a user edit, in the SAME pass, right before the dedup step. It decrypts via the Vault (the one reconcile pass that holds keys) through the same decryptError gate the read path uses, and persists the tag LOCAL-ONLY (apply_row, no updated_at bump) — matching the oracle byte-for-byte and avoiding a whole-row-LWW clobber; convergence rides the dedup soft-delete (which does propagate). The merge-PROMPT UI half stays client-surface (SUR-759 / SUR-819).
Core reconciliation helper — same behavior as the preserve-dropped-tags step (SUR-597 -> core SUR-820).

Client-initiated book-merge UI action; native clients converge on its SYNCED effects (soft-deleted losers, rehomed notes, merge map) via core post-pull reconciliation (SUR-820). Per-platform merge UI deferred to SUR-759 / SUR-819. RATIFY: waiver vs a native merge-UI port ticket.
Client-initiated undo-merge UI; synced effects converge via core reconciliation (SUR-820). Per-platform UI deferred to SUR-759 / SUR-819. RATIFY: waiver vs port ticket.

Client-initiated note-merge UI; synced effects (edge + collection-membership rehome) converge via core reconciliation (SUR-820). Per-platform UI deferred to SUR-759 / SUR-819. RATIFY: waiver vs port ticket.
Core post-pull reconciliation helper backing the rehome-stranded-notes step.
Core sync engine — LWW cloud->local merge across the 8 synced tables (SUR-659 / SUR-725).

note_signals birth-row writer (SUR-615). note_signals ROWS sync and are received via core merge-cloud-records; native signal COLLECTION (writing signals on interaction) is not yet ported. RATIFY: waiver vs a note_signals native-collection port ticket.
note_signals exposure-recency writer (SUR-615); native signal collection not yet ported — received via core merge-cloud-records. RATIFY: waiver vs port ticket.
note_signals engagement-recency writer (SUR-615); native signal collection not yet ported — received via core merge-cloud-records. RATIFY: waiver vs port ticket.
note_signals return-visit writer (SUR-615); native signal collection not yet ported — received via core merge-cloud-records. RATIFY: waiver vs port ticket.

Core margins op — replace_handwritten_annotations read-merge-stages the parent's has_annotation=true (whole row, counters preserved, importance recomputed, PWA change-detection no-op) in the same stage_local_writes batch as the child notes/edges. The set-FALSE half is SUR-959.

note_signals hasAnnotation reconciler vs live note_links edges. The set-TRUE leg ships with record-annotation (SUR-956, the margins op — which never ends with zero margins); the recompute-to-FALSE leg lands with the future margins-delete path, ticketed SUR-959.
note_signals stitch-spawn writer (SUR-615); native signal collection not yet ported — received via core merge-cloud-records. RATIFY: waiver vs port ticket.

note_signals tombstone-on-note-delete writer (SUR-615). Cross-device tail: the oracle ALWAYS emits a tombstone (even with no local signal row) to tear down a signal row another device created — the eventual note_signals port MUST include this delete-tombstone leg or orphaned signal rows linger (benign metadata leak, not data loss). Native signal collection not yet ported; rows received via core merge-cloud-records. RATIFY: waiver vs port ticket.
